post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 256
UIC Invitational VI: Mentors



An insurgency is rising against the unforgiving empire of Theom. There are rumors whispered in the shadows and plans hatched in noisy bars, but above them all march the anima soldiers of the empire. Stronger, faster, and impervious to all known weapons forged by man, the anima annihilate any who stand in the way. Theom was forged on the backs of its soldiers, and as long as its anima have no equal, it would not fall. To bring down an empire, you must first dismantle its engine.

Perhaps the secret lies within the network of caverns twisting deep underneath the capital, where the shattered remnants of hundreds anima drift, broken off from their other halves...


Please the following three tabs. They've been shortened as much as possible and contain critical information.

- Important Rules -
Integrity
  • Do not OOT and talk to your neighbor or mentors outside given private chats.
  • Do not use OGI (Out of Game Information) or angleshoot based on mentors. Reads should be made on the players.

Activity
  • Each "Day" is 12 days while each "Night" is 2 days.
  • This game uses a Pregame phase: all parties will have 24 hours to coordinate in their private chats before the thread opens.
  • There is no confirm rule.
  • All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable.
  • Activity rules are 48 hours to first PM prod and 72 hours to second PM prod. Replacements will be addressed as needed.

Voting
  • Both bold vote and code vote formats are accepted, although bold is preferred.
  • Majority hang is always on.
  • Deadline hang is always on.
  • Votes are not locked at LYLO.

Etiquette and Warnings
  • You may attack a player’s opinion, but do not make personal attacks on players themselves.
  • Do not discuss rule violations by other players in the thread; contact the moderators privately. This applies to any complaints about other players and mentors as well.
  • All players can receive up to 2 warnings. Modkills and force replacements are solely up to the moderators.

Setup and Moderators
  • This game is based on DkKoba's Would You Like To Be My Neighbor setup.
  • All parties with private chats have daychat.
  • This game is moderated by Mathletesv and Scipio1.
  • This game operates optional discord spectator and mafia private chats using the Unpaid Intern Central server. Contact the mods to request access.

Resources and Support
  • For any issues, you may contact us through your role PM or through Discord (@Mathletesv#6308).
  • If this game is causing you significant stress or frustration, feel free to use your role PM to talk or vent. We may not be allowed to give you technical advice, but we can listen or help you find a better way to process events. You may also contact us via Discord if you prefer.

- Setup -
This game will run a version of DkKoba's Would You Like To Be My Neighbor setup, modified for a mentor game format. This is a 9p setup with the following roles:
Roles wrote:
  • 6 Town Neighbors
  • 1 Town 1-Shot Friendly Neighbor
  • 2 Mafia Neighbors

The 1-Shot Friendly Neighbor has the ability privately inform a single player that they are mod-confirmed town at any point in the game. The other 8 Neighbors are divided into private neighborhoods as follows:
Neighborhood Distribution wrote:
  • Town Neighbor 1 + Town Neighbor 2
  • Town Neighbor 3 + Town Neighbor 4
  • Mafia Neighbor 1 + Town Neighbor 5
  • Mafia Neighbor 2 + Town Neighbor 6

The Town Neighbors are not informed which # neighbor they are, and as a result will not know whether they are in a neighborhood with town or mafia. Here are the full role PMs:

Role PMs
Town Neighbor Role PM wrote:


Hello, [player]. Welcome to UIC Invitational VI: Mentors.

You are an Insurgent Researcher (Town Neighbor). With your team, you ventured deep underneath the Citadel and into the Catacombs, in search of a way to help the Insurgents win against Theom's anima soldiers. Unfortunately, Arol spies infiltrated your mission and almost killed your entire group in an explosion.

You were saved by [Mentor 1], a Relict Anima (Neighborhood Mentor). [For 2 mentors: You were saved by [Mentor 1] and [Mentor 2], Relict Anima (Neighborhood Mentors).] Together with [Neighbor], you may talk privately to each other in this chat as long as you are alive. You do not know your neighbor's alignment.

Your wincondition is to find and eliminate all Arol spies. Good luck.
Town 1-Shot Friendly Neighbor Role PM wrote:


Hello, [player]. Welcome to UIC Invitational VI: Mentors.

You are an Insurgent Alchemist (Town 1-Shot Friendly Neighbor). With your team, you ventured deep underneath the Citadel and into the Catacombs, in search of a way to help the Insurgents win against Theom's anima soldiers. Unfortunately, Arol spies infiltrated your mission and almost killed your entire group in an explosion.

You were saved by [Mentor], a Relict Anima (Town Mentor), who you may talk to privately in this chat as long as you are alive.

In addition, you have a single dose of Jesl truth potion, which you can use on yourself to privately prove to a single player that you are town. You may activate this ability once during the game, at any time, and the recipient will informed as soon as moderator availability allows.

Your wincondition is to find and eliminate all Arol spies. Good luck.
Mafia Neighbor Role PM wrote:


Hello, [player]. Welcome to UIC Invitational VI: Mentors.

You are an Arol Spy (Mafia Neighbor). After receiving intelligence that insurgents against the empire were working to decipher the secrets behind the anima, you worked to insert yourself into their research team, posing as a scholar from Myohan. You joined them on their mission into the Catacombs where you stumbled upon a relict anima, who agreed to help you. With a escape plan in place, you and your partner set off a massive explosion. Unfortunately, the relict anima that you found was not the only one in the area, and you are now trapped in a cavern with 7 insurgents.

You were "saved" by [Mentor 1], a Relict Anima (Neighborhood Mentor). [For 2 mentors: You were "saved" by [Mentor 1] and [Mentor 2], Relict Anima (Neighborhood Mentors).] Together with [Neighbor], you may talk privately to each other in this chat as long as you are alive. Your neighbor is an insurgent, and your mentor does not know that you are a spy.

In addition, you have a secret chat that you share with your partner [Mafia 2], an Arol Spy (Mafia Neighbor), and [Mafia Mentor(s)], the Relict Anima (Mafia Mentor) who agreed to help you. You may talk privately to each other in this chat as long as you are alive. Each night, your relict anima can shroud you in darkness just long enough for you or your partner to kill one person.

Your wincondition is to eliminate all of the insurgents. Good luck.
Neighborhood Mentor Role PM wrote:


Hello, [mentor]. Welcome to UIC Invitational VI: Mentors.

You are a Relict Anima (Neighborhood Mentor), an anima clinging onto existence after your Achates died in the Dikhotohm. You were thrown into the Catacombs an unknown number of years ago where you drifted aimlessly until you sensed someone in mortal danger.

You chose to save [Neighbor 1] and [Neighbor 2], both Neighbors of unknown alignment. You may talk to them privately in this chat as long as they are alive. You are a mentor, and cannot post in the thread.

The mafia mentor is [Mafia Mentor]. Do not share this information with your mentees. Your wincondition is to help your mentees develop as better mafia players, whatever alignment they are. Good luck.
Friendly Neighbor Mentor Role PM wrote:


Hello, [mentor]. Welcome to UIC Invitational VI: Mentors.

You are a Relict Anima (Town Mentor), an anima clinging onto existence after your Achates died in the Dikhotohm. You were thrown into the Catacombs an unknown number of years ago where you drifted aimlessly until you sensed someone in mortal danger.

You chose to save [Town Friendly Neighbor], the Insurgent Alchemist (Town 1-Shot Friendly Neighbor). You may talk to them privately in this chat as long as they are alive. You are a mentor, and cannot post in the thread.

The mafia mentor is [Mafia Mentor]. Do not share this information with your mentees. Your wincondition is to help your mentee develop as a better mafia players and eliminate all Arol spies. Good luck.
Mafia Mentor Role PM wrote:


Hello, [mentor]. Welcome to UIC Invitational VI: Mentors.

You are a Relict Anima (Mafia Mentor), an anima clinging onto existence after your Achates died in the Dikhotohm. You were thrown into the Catacombs an unknown number of years ago where you drifted aimlessly until an Arol spy found the broken sword you were anchored to and yanked you back. It's been so long since you've felt an Achates's warmth... you just agreed to whatever they wanted, to whisk them to safety after they set off the explosion. But something went wrong.

The Arol spies are [Mafia 1] and [Mafia 2]. You may talk to them in the mafia factional chat as long as they are alive. They are also each in two separate neighborhood chats that you do not have access to. You are a mentor, and cannot post in the thread.

Only the other mentors have been informed that you are the mafia mentor. Your wincondition is to help your mentees develop as better mafia players and eliminate all of the insurgents. Good luck.


There will be 6 total groups of mentors in this game. The groups are as follows:
Mentor Groups wrote:
  • chess24
  • EulerNTM
  • fdas + midwaybear
  • mathjay
  • NDMath + sudzoof
  • Osmium_

Each group will be assigned to mentor a neighborhood, with the 2 remaining groups mentoring the 1-Shot Friendly Neighbor and the mafia factional chat. The mentors cannot post in the game thread and essentially function as a neutral party; everyone except the mafia chat mentor(s) do not know who the mafia are. The identity of the scumchat mentor is not public and will be shared only with the other mentors.

There are some big Do's and Don'ts regarding mentors to ensure everything goes smoothly, so please read the following tab carefully.

- Mentor Game Specific Rules -
For all parties: Keep it respectful.
This should not be an issue but it always bears saying. Respectful discussion is the name of this game.


For the people being mentored:
  • You are not allowed to speculate on the mentors' identities in the thread. Do not talk about your guesses for who you think is mentoring who. Even if this factors into your reads, do not mention this in the thread. Mentioning it in private chats is permissible, but keep discussion limited.
  • Do not directly quote your mentors. Even if you wish to tell the thread about what your mentors said, paraphrase.
  • Do not quote verbatim from your neighborhood in general. You are allowed to reveal who you are in a neighborhood with, although you should discuss it with your neighbor.

Notes about the neighborhood-mentor format:
  • Your mentors are there to help you, but they are not there to play the game for you. Think of them as someone trustworthy who can answer any questions you have about your reads, the game, the threadstate, etc.
  • Your mentors are also great players with different specialties. Treat this as a learning experience. You are allowed to disagree with your mentors, but it should be a discussion.
  • Your mentors are not allowed to accuse their mentees of being scum. That said, if you think your neighbor partner is scum, you are allowed to bring this up with your shared mentors. Your neighbor partner will presumably attempt to argue against your case; just keep the discussion civil and don't expect your mentors to choose sides.
  • If you are mafia and are being suspected in your neighborhood chat, you should absolutely bring this up with your scumchat mentor, who will try to help you.

For the neighborhood mentors:
  • You do not have a wincon. You are effectively a neutral party.

An example to clarify: if you suspect that one of your mentees is scum, you are not allowed to attempt to out them or tunnel them in your neighborhood until they break under pressure. This is acknowledged to be a weakness of the neighborhood-mentor format, and it is up to your discretion how you wish to handle this issue. Always keep in mind that your objective this game is to challenge and guide your mentees to improve as players.

As such, you should not "out" one of your mentees because they are still your mentee, and they are there to learn from you. You should never be a hostile entity and make it harder for them to approach you for your help, especially if you are not 100% certain that they are mafia. (If someone ends up being outed, disregard this statement.) Note that even if one of your mentees is a wolf, your feedback helps them become more convincingly towny (and if your reads are wrong you will probably be milked for cred).

If one of your mentees scumreads the other and asks for your thoughts, that opens up the discussion. You can respond however you see fit; you could give an honest answer, or evaluate their case while being evasive of what you think. This situation is basically guaranteed to pop up, so deal with any neighborhood thunderdomes as gracefully as you can. It doesn't matter if you scumside or townside; do as you want and just keep your neighborhood always open for discussion. If something goes wrong, blame mathjay for the format postgame.


The rest of the rules don't matter, they're standard rules and are there for peace of mind. Good luck.

  • General
    Credits to Binomial-theorem, chaotic_iak, willwang123, and fdas, among others, as well as the Mafia Universe forum rules, for the rule template.

    The following are standard mafia game rules.

    • Do not post during the night phase.
    • Do not talk about this game's events outside of this thread unless you are specifically given permission to do so by the moderators. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread. Referring to anything that has happened in an ongoing game, even if you do not say which game it is, is OOT.
    • If you are dead, do not post, and do not talk about this game anywhere else. You may still spectate; access to spoiled and unspoiled spectator chats will be offered.
    • Do not use any formatting that obscures or conceals text. This includes [color=#FFFFFF], [color=white], and [color=transparent].
    • Do not talk in languages other than English.
    • Do not edit or delete posts, even for typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON as appropriate.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from the mods, including role PMs.
    • Do not impersonate the mods.
    • Do not lie to the mods.
    • Do not abuse loopholes in the rules. Intent will be considered during rule enforcement.
    • You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications.

  • Votes and Hangs
    This tab covers voting, unvoting, No Hangs, and hangs.

    • Votes must be in this format to be counted: Vote: [player username].
    • By default, votes should be made in [b] (bold) tags. However, votes made in [code] and [c] tags will also be accepted.
    • Usernames in votes must be spelled, but not necessarily capitalized, correctly. Do not use nicknames.
    • For the purposes of voting, I (capital i) and l (lowercase L) are considered identical.
    • For example, both Vote: mathjay and Vote: mAtHjAy are valid votes, but vote: mathjay, Vote: jay, Vote: mathjay., Vote:mathjay, Vote: mathjay, and Vote: mathjay are invalid.
    • Votes followed by extraneous punctuation that is similarly formatted, even with separate tags, are invalid.
    • Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags do not count.
    • Vote to hang no one by posting Vote: No Hang.
    • Unvote by posting Unvote. You do not have to unvote before voting again.
    • Votecounts will be posted periodically. Players may request a new VC in bold or in their role PM.
    • Invalid votes will not count under any circumstances. Consider notifying the mods in your role PM if you want to purposely fakevote.
    • Valid votes will always be counted unless they are clearly unintentional. There is no guarantee that an accidental vote will not be counted.
    • Votes will be carried over if a player is replaced.
    • The Not Voting section should be ignored if it contradicts another part of the votecount (VC).
    • If an error in the votecount is not pointed out within 2 days or by the next EOD, whichever is sooner, the incorrect VC will become official.
    • This game uses deadline hang: the player with the most votes at the EOD (End of Day) deadline will be hanged.
    • If two or more players have the same number of votes at the EOD deadline, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • If no votes have been cast by the deadline, no one will be hanged.
    • From the start of the game, if a player is voted by the majority of living players, then that player will be hanged immediately; and if No Hang is voted by half of living players, a No Hang will occur.
    • Votes will still be unlocked at LYLO (Hang-or-Lose).
    • The moment the deadline occurs, no further votes will be counted. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • If the deadline has passed or a hammer has occurred and the EOD has not been declared, the game will proceed into twilight. Players may still post, but no more votes or unvotes will be counted.
    • Once the moderators declare the EOD, the person hanged will be officially eliminated and their role revealed. Night will then immediately begin.
  • Activity
    This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.

    • All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
    • Each day phase will be 288 hours (12 days), and each night phase will be 48 hours (2 days).
    • The moderators may extend deadlines at their discretion.
    • The current deadline can be found in the bottom of the OP (Original Post, or post #1).
    • If a player with an NA (Night Action) anticipates being unavailable during the night phase, they may submit any night actions to be performed in advance.
    • If a player with an NA has not submitted their action by 24 hours before the night deadline, a reminder will be sent.
    • If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform an action that night.
    • Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
    • Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night (EON). You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates if they miss a deadline.
    • Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence) by posting the V/LA dates in bold in-thread or in their role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded.
    • V/LAs should be shorter than one week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for options.
    • Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request replacement by posting Request Replacement in-thread or in their role PM.
    • Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in.
    • Players will receive their first PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Players will receive their second PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA.
    • Players will be replaced at the moderators' discretion. In some cases, mentors may step in as replacements for their mentees.
    • Activity Checks (ACs) will be posted periodically. Players may request a new AC in bold or in their role PM.
    • Activity will reset overnight. Players will not receive prods between twilight and daystart.
    • If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. The moderators will send a notification via PM to the specified player, but this will not count as an official prod.
    • If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
  • Integrity and Etiquette
    This tab covers integrity recommendations, player etiquette, warning limits, and examples of inconsiderate gameplay.

    • Do not gamethrow.
    • Do not discuss player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, or other interactions with moderators.
    • Do not discuss rule violations, especially violations by other players in the thread. If you wish, contact the moderators privately through your role PM or Discord.
    • Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive player-to-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.
    • Do not publicly reference OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or create situations outside the thread that players could reasonably consider alignment indicative.

    The above rules are attempts to preserve the spirit of the game and should not affect most players. For specific details and examples, click here.

    • Do not make personal attacks. You may attack a player's opinion, but do not demean the player themselves.
    • Do not spam.
    • Follow AoPS Community Guidelines and the AoPS Terms of Service.
    • Be mature and use common sense.
    • Behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning.
      Causing significant disruptions to gameplay may result in a force replacement at the moderators' discretion.
    • For minor rule infractions, first-timers can receive up to three warnings, while all other players can receive up to two. A player may receive multiple warnings for a single infraction.

    The above rules broadly address most etiquette issues. For specific details and examples of improper behavior, click here.

  • - Moderation Policies -
  • Moderator Information
    This tab provides technical information about this game.

    • Mathletesv and Scipio1 are the moderators of Mafia Game 256.
    • Other Unpaid Interns have contributed to flavor, setup, OCRSTM, feedback, and management of the private chat discord system.
    • This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be granted access as appropriate.
    • Red text is used for votecounts, warnings, and other announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related text.
    • Blue text is used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators.
    • Green text is used for town flavor-related text.
    • Purple text is used for third-party flavor-related text.
    • Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes.
    • Current custom shades are: Town flavor green v.2 (#2be368) and Activity Check blue (#005dff). For a full list of custom colors, click here.
    • The moderators may change the rules at any time. Major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OCRSTM Version History.
    • Players will not be retroactively penalized for violating new rules.
    • Major changes to the OP may be made in between games. The most important changes will be noted in the OCRSTM Version History.
    • The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make the final judgement in the moderation of this game.

    MG256 OP is based on Overcomplicated RulesetTM (OCRSTM) 10.5
    Current version: 10.5.0
    Version History

    OCRSTM 10 is available for public use. If you are interested in using OCRSTM in your own games, click here.

There will be 6 total groups of mentors in this game. The groups are as follows: 

[quote=Mentor Groups]
[list][*]chess24
[*]EulerNTM
[*]fdas + midwaybear
[*]mathjay
[*]NDMath + sudzoof
[*]Osmium_[/list]
[/quote]

Each group will be assigned to mentor a neighborhood, with the 2 remaining groups mentoring the 1-Shot Friendly Neighbor and the mafia factional chat.  The mentors [b]cannot post in the game thread[/b] and essentially function as a [b]neutral party[/b]; everyone except the mafia chat mentor(s) do not know who the mafia are. The identity of the scumchat mentor is not public and will be shared only with the other mentors. 

There are some big Do's and Don'ts regarding mentors to ensure everything goes smoothly, so please read the following tab carefully. 
[/hide]
[hide=- Mentor Game Specific Rules -]
[b]For all parties: Keep it respectful. [/b]
This should not be an issue but it always bears saying. Respectful discussion is the name of this game. 


[b]For the people being mentored:[/b]

[list][*][b]You are not allowed to speculate on the mentors' identities in the thread.[/b] Do not talk about your guesses for who you think is mentoring who. Even if this factors into your reads, do not mention this in the thread. Mentioning it in private chats is permissible, but keep discussion limited. 
[*][b]Do not directly quote your mentors.[/b] Even if you wish to tell the thread about what your mentors said, paraphrase. 
[*][b]Do not quote verbatim from your neighborhood in general.[/b] You [i]are[/i] allowed to reveal who you are in a neighborhood with, although you should discuss it with your neighbor. 
[/list]

Notes about the neighborhood-mentor format: 
[list][*]Your mentors are there to help you, but they are not there to play the game for you. Think of them as someone trustworthy who can answer any questions you have about your reads, the game, the threadstate, etc. 
[*]Your mentors are also great players with different specialties. Treat this as a learning experience. You are allowed to disagree with your mentors, but it should be a discussion. 
[*]Your mentors are not allowed to accuse their mentees of being scum. That said, if you think your neighbor partner is scum, you [i]are[/i] allowed to bring this up with your shared mentors. Your neighbor partner will presumably attempt to argue against your case; just keep the discussion civil and don't expect your mentors to choose sides. 
[*]If you are mafia and are being suspected in your neighborhood chat, you should absolutely bring this up with your scumchat mentor, who will try to help you. [/list]

[b]For the neighborhood mentors:[/b]

[list][*][b]You do not have a wincon. You are effectively a neutral party.[/b] [/list]

An example to clarify: if you suspect that one of your mentees is scum, you are not allowed to attempt to out them or tunnel them in your neighborhood until they break under pressure. This is acknowledged to be a weakness of the neighborhood-mentor format, and it is up to your discretion how you wish to handle this issue. Always keep in mind that your objective this game is to challenge and guide your mentees to improve as players. 

As such, you should not "out" one of your mentees because they are still your mentee, and they are there to learn from you. You should never be a hostile entity and make it harder for them to approach you for your help, especially if you are not 100% certain that they are mafia. (If someone ends up being outed, disregard this statement.) Note that even if one of your mentees is a wolf, your feedback helps them become more convincingly towny (and if your reads are wrong you will probably be milked for cred). 

If one of your mentees scumreads the other and asks for your thoughts, that opens up the discussion. You can respond however you see fit; you could give an honest answer, or evaluate their case while being evasive of what you think. This situation is basically guaranteed to pop up, so deal with any neighborhood thunderdomes as gracefully as you can. It doesn't matter if you scumside or townside; do as you want and just keep your neighborhood always open for discussion. If something goes wrong, blame mathjay for the format postgame. 


[b]The rest of the rules don't matter, they're standard rules and are there for peace of mind. Good luck. [/b]
[/hide]

[list][*][hide=General]
[i]Credits to [url=http://bit.ly/fbith]Binomial-theorem[/url], [url=http://bit.ly/rmchao]chaotic_iak[/url], [url=http://bit.ly/rmww123]willwang123[/url], and [url=http://bit.ly/ffdas]fdas[/url], among others, as well as the [url=http://bit.ly/MUnFR]Mafia Universe forum rules[/url], for the rule template.[/i]

[b]The following are standard mafia game rules.[/b]


[list][*]Do not post during the night phase.
[*]Do not talk about this game's events outside of this thread unless you are specifically given permission to do so by the moderators. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread. Referring to anything that has happened in an ongoing game, even if you do not say which game it is, is OOT.
[*]If you are dead, do not post, and do not talk about this game anywhere else. You may still spectate; access to spoiled and unspoiled spectator chats will be offered. 
[*]Do not use any formatting that obscures or conceals text. This includes [verbatim][color=#FFFFFF], [color=white], and [color=transparent][/verbatim]. 
[*]Do not talk in languages other than English.
[*]Do not edit or delete posts, even for typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON as appropriate. 
[*]Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from the mods, including role PMs.
[*]Do not impersonate the mods. 
[*]Do not lie to the mods. 
[*]Do not abuse loopholes in the rules. Intent will be considered during rule enforcement. 
[*]You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications.[/list][/hide]

[*][hide=Votes and Hangs]
[b]This tab covers voting, unvoting, No Hangs, and hangs.[/b]


[list][*]Votes must be in this format to be counted: [b]Vote: [player username][/b].
[*]By default, votes should be made in [verbatim][b] (bold) tags. However, votes made in [code] and [c] tags will also be accepted.[/verbatim]
[*]Usernames in votes must be spelled, but not necessarily capitalized, correctly. Do not use nicknames. 
[*]For the purposes of voting, I (capital i) and l (lowercase L) are considered identical. 
[*]For example, both [b]Vote: mathjay[/b] and [b]Vote: mAtHjAy[/b] are valid votes, but [b]vote: mathjay[/b], [b]Vote: jay[/b], [b]Vote: mathjay.[/b], [b]Vote:mathjay[/b], Vote: [b]mathjay[/b], and [b][i]Vote: mathjay[/i][/b] are invalid. 
[*]Votes followed by extraneous punctuation that is similarly formatted, even with separate tags, are invalid. 
[*]Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags do not count.
[*]Vote to hang no one by posting [b]Vote: No Hang[/b].
[*]Unvote by posting [b]Unvote[/b]. You do not have to unvote before voting again. 
[*]Votecounts will be posted periodically. Players may request a new VC in bold or in their role PM. 
[*]Invalid votes will not count under any circumstances. Consider notifying the mods in your role PM if you want to purposely fakevote.
[*]Valid votes will always be counted unless they are clearly unintentional. There is no guarantee that an accidental vote will not be counted.
[*]Votes will be carried over if a player is replaced. 
[*]The Not Voting section should be ignored if it contradicts another part of the votecount (VC). 
[*]If an error in the votecount is not pointed out within 2 days or by the next EOD, whichever is sooner, the incorrect VC will become official.
[*]This game uses deadline hang: the player with the most votes at the EOD (End of Day) deadline will be hanged.
[*]If two or more players have the same number of votes at the EOD deadline, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]If no votes have been cast by the deadline, no one will be hanged. 
[*]From the start of the game, if a player is voted by the majority of living players, then that player will be hanged immediately; and if No Hang is voted by half of living players, a No Hang will occur.
[*]Votes will still be unlocked at LYLO (Hang-or-Lose).
[*]The moment the deadline occurs, no further votes will be counted. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]If the deadline has passed or a hammer has occurred and the EOD has not been declared, the game will proceed into twilight. Players may still post, but no more votes or unvotes will be counted.
[*]Once the moderators declare the EOD, the person hanged will be officially eliminated and their role revealed. Night will then immediately begin.[/list][/hide]
[*][hide=Activity]
[b]This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.[/b]


[list][*]All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
[*]Each day phase will be 288 hours (12 days), and each night phase will be 48 hours (2 days). 
[*]The moderators may extend deadlines at their discretion.
[*]The current deadline can be found in the bottom of the OP (Original Post, or post #1). 
[*]If a player with an NA (Night Action) anticipates being unavailable during the night phase, they may submit any night actions to be performed in advance. 
[*]If a player with an NA has not submitted their action by 24 hours before the night deadline, a reminder will be sent. 
[*]If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform an action that night.
[*]Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
[*]Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night (EON). You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates if they miss a deadline. 
[*]Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence) by posting the V/LA dates in [b]bold[/b] in-thread or in their role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded. 
[*]V/LAs should be shorter than one week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for options. 
[*]Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request replacement by posting [b]Request Replacement[/b] in-thread or in their role PM. 
[*]Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in. 
[*]Players will receive their first PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
[*]Players will receive their second PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA. 
[*]Players will be replaced at the moderators' discretion. In some cases, mentors may step in as replacements for their mentees. 
[*]Activity Checks (ACs) will be posted periodically. Players may request a new AC in bold or in their role PM. 
[*]Activity will reset overnight. Players will not receive prods between twilight and daystart. 
[*]If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. The moderators will send a notification via PM to the specified player, but this will not count as an official prod.
[*]If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.[list][/hide]
[*][hide=Integrity and Etiquette]
[b]This tab covers integrity recommendations, player etiquette, warning limits, and examples of inconsiderate gameplay.[/b]


[list][*]Do not gamethrow. 
[*]Do not discuss player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, or other interactions with moderators. 
[*]Do not discuss rule violations, especially violations by other players in the thread. If you wish, contact the moderators privately through your role PM or Discord. 
[*]Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive player-to-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.
[*]Do not publicly reference OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or create situations outside the thread that players could reasonably consider alignment indicative.[/list]

The above rules are attempts to preserve the spirit of the game and should not affect most players. For specific details and examples, [url=https://aops.com/community/p24617334]click here[/url]. 


[list][*]Do not make personal attacks. You may attack a player's opinion, but do not demean the player themselves. 
[*]Do not spam.
[*]Follow AoPS Community Guidelines and the AoPS Terms of Service.
[*]Be mature and use common sense.
[*]Behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning.
[b]Causing significant disruptions to gameplay may result in a force replacement at the moderators' discretion. 
[*]For minor rule infractions, first-timers can receive up to three warnings, while all other players can receive up to two. A player may receive multiple warnings for a single infraction.[/list]

The above rules broadly address most etiquette issues. For specific details and examples of improper behavior, [url=https://aops.com/community/p24617342]click here[/url].[/hide]

[*][b][url=https://aops.com/community/p24617357]- Moderation Policies -[/url][/b]

[*][hide=Moderator Information]
[b]This tab provides technical information about this game.[/b]


[list][*]Mathletesv and Scipio1 are the moderators of Mafia Game 256. 
[*]Other Unpaid Interns have contributed to flavor, setup, OCRSTM, feedback, and management of the private chat discord system.
[*]This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be granted access as appropriate.
[*][color=#FF0000]Red text[/color] is used for votecounts, warnings, and other announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related text.
[*][color=#0000FF]Blue text[/color] is used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators. 
[*][color=#00FF00]Green text[/color] is used for town flavor-related text.
[*][color=#9a00ff]Purple text[/color] is used for third-party flavor-related text.
[*]Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes. 
[*]Current custom shades are: Town flavor green v.2 ([b][color=#2be368]#2be368[/color][/b]) and Activity Check blue ([b][color=#005dff]#005dff[/color][/b]). For a full list of custom colors, [url=https://aops.com/community/p24617373]click here[/url]. 
[*]The moderators may change the rules at any time. Major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OCRSTM Version History. 
[*]Players will not be retroactively penalized for violating new rules. 
[*]Major changes to the OP may be made in between games. The most important changes will be noted in the OCRSTM Version History. 
[*]The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make the final judgement in the moderation of this game.[/list]

[b]MG256 OP is based on Overcomplicated Ruleset[sup]TM[/sup] (OCRSTM) 10.5[/b]

post_rendered:
You wake up on a cold stone floor with sharp rocks digging into your back. It's so dark that it's hard to see anything, the only light coming from a few crystal snails glowing on the walls.

A cat made up of billowing clouds of light slips out of a crack in the rocks. It climbs up on top of one of the big chunks of rubble littering the cavern floor, casting a cool glow over the debris. It meows once, before starting to groom itself.

Someone gasps. "An anima..."

The cat looks up. "A broken one, sure."

Multiple people gasp.

The cat sighs. "Why are you so surprised? You're all acting like someone who's never seen one before."

Silence.

The cat blinks, its eyes glowing like lamps. "Nevermind. You should know that two amongst you set off the explosion that collapsed the cavern you were in. That's all I can tell you." It raises one paw. A red shackle briefly appears around it, flashing when tugged. The cat does not look happy about it. "I have been bound to a covenant, as you can see.

"Choose someone to die by the day's eve. Thus are the terms of the covenant."

The cat dissipates into the air, the glow fading away.

Mathletesv is a Mysterious Relict Anima (Moderator 1). He was bound to a covenant by an unknown entity.

Day 1 has started. You may now post.
